design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate themed platformer levels based on Looney Tunes shorts, defeat stage bosses (classic antagonists), reach final confrontation with Daffy Duck
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (carrots become scarce as game progresses) combined with increasing enemy complexity and boss pattern recognition
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as Bugs Bunny outsmarting and outmaneuvering iconic Looney Tunes villains through platforming skill and comedic attacks (pies, kicks, spin attacks)
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: License-driven design leveraging Looney Tunes IP with level theming based on specific classic shorts; emphasizes character variety over mechanical depth; narrative frames gameplay as Bugs trapped in Daffy's painting

difficulty.lives_system: Health tracked as carrot meter; regainable through finding carrot pickups
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Minimal explicit difficulty selection documented; challenge scales through enemy projectile frequency, enemy types (pigs with slingshots, robots, black cats triggering crushing), and boss pattern complexity

progression.upgrade_system: Consumable items including health carrots and special one-hit-kill/stun items found throughout levels
progression.unlock_mechanism: Standard stage-to-stage progression; final level features unique Daffy Duck encounter with phase structure (paint spilling objective, then boss fight variations)
replayability_hooks: Varied level types (left-to-right, descending navigation, different enemy arrangements)
Boss encounter learning (Toro the Bull, Tasmanian Devil require pattern recognition)
Carrot scarcity increases challenge on replays

world_building_techniques: Level theming tied to specific 1950s Looney Tunes shorts (Bully for Bugs, etc.)
Boss characters draw from canonical Looney Tunes antagonists (Elmer Fudd, Marvin the Martian, Yosemite Sam, Wile E. Coyote, Tasmanian Devil)
Framing narrative: Bugs trapped in painting by Daffy Duck; ending sequence with post-credits appearance and explosive finale
Voice acting (Greg Burson) for character authenticity

boss_design.philosophy: Boss encounters require observational pattern learning and spatial problem-solving; bosses are recognizable Looney Tunes characters with attack patterns tied to their cartoon personas
notable_bosses: Tasmanian Devil (Taz) (level boss requiring pattern learning)
Toro the Bull (from Bully for Bugs short, requires outsmarting and maneuvering)
Drip-Along Daffy (final boss variant if not all paint spilled)
Robin Hood Daffy (final boss variant if all paint spilled)
Duck Dodgers (invincible final-level obstacle with one-hit-kill laser; must be avoided)

faction_indicators: Bugs Bunny as protagonist/hero against canonical Looney Tunes villains
Daffy Duck as primary antagonist (animator controlling the painted world)
Enemy types: pigs (slingshot users), robots (electrical hazard), black cats (superstition mechanics)
danger_signals: Black cats trigger crushing hazards (safes, horses) when crossed
Projectile-spam enemies (pigs with slingshots) scale in frequency
Duck Dodgers' laser blaster is instant death
Carrot scarcity increases as game progresses, reducing health recovery options
environmental_storytelling: Painted world aesthetic reinforces Daffy's narrative control
Level designs mimic cartoon short settings and hazards
Boss encounters embody character personalities from source material (Toro's charging, Taz's spinning)
